title: polemonium caeruleum subsp. caucasicum
intro: Polemonium caeruleum subsp. caucasicum, also known as Caucasian Jacob's-ladder, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Polemonium genus. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Polemonium caeruleum subsp. caucasicum typically involve infusions or decoctions of its leaves and roots, which can be consumed as teas or used as topical applications.
